hermes.goal: Beat buy-and-hold (positive alpha vs BTC) while keeping max drawdown under 15% and never risking the mandate on a single trade — capital preservation first. Near-term: prove a sustained, fee-aware, positive out-of-sample edge on the paper trainer over ~90 days before scaling any live capital; grow the live account only on demonstrated alpha.

brain.commentary: All four crypto signals are flat with negative composites and alphas. Betas are at or above 1 for three assets and volatilities range from 0.38 to 0.55, indicating elevated draw‑down risk. BTC shows the most benign beta/vol profile, so a modest positive tilt is acceptable. ETH, SOL and LTC carry higher beta or volatility and negative alpha; SOL and LTC have the weakest composites and are flagged as fragile, so we trim their convictions more aggressively. The overall stance is defensive, scaling the crypto slice to 70% of the baseline exposure.
